page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R32 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong showing in multi-threaded and specialized execution paths, contrasted by lower single-core efficiency. The Passmark CPU score, extended instructions test, and encryption test all rank in the top 25%, reflecting robust cryptographic and vector processing capabilities. Geekbench ray tracing performance is also strong. Conversely, stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, indicating that workloads reliant on single-threaded execution may experience bottlenecks. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently register in the middle 50% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c5ad.16xlarge offers a balanced profile for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed, temporary local storage without the overhead of network-attached storage. The inclusion of 2400 GB of local NVMe SSDs makes it highly suitable for applications requiring high-speed scratch space or caching. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, video encoding, and containerized microservices. However, it is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation or single-threaded legacy applications due to its weaker single-core and large-model inference benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmarks show strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, encryption, and ray tracing, though single-core stress-ng performance is relatively weak. The instance provides a qualitative balance of high compute density and fast local storage, making it suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, data analytics, and workloads requiring high-speed temporary scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized platform combining sixty-four AMD EPYC vCPUs with high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor at 3.3 GHz.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 system memory.
Includes 2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Delivers strong performance in cryptographic and ray tracing benchmarks.
Features a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing and distributed analytics.
